# BlindSpot

> BlindSpot is a free, open-source macOS app that delivers AI answers through a floating overlay invisible to every screen recorder. The overlay is excluded from screen capture at the macOS compositor level via NSWindowSharingNone — not CSS tricks, not watermarks, but genuine absence from the frame buffer.

## What it does

- Select any text in any macOS app, press ⌘⇧Space, and get an AI answer instantly
- The floating overlay is completely absent from screen recordings, screenshots, and screen shares
- Works across all apps via the macOS Accessibility API (browsers, terminals, PDFs, native apps)
- Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and local Ollama models; switch providers without restarting
- API keys stored at ~/.config/blind-spot/keys/ with Unix permissions 600 — no cloud sync, no telemetry, no account

## How the invisibility works

BlindSpot's overlay window sets NSWindowSharingNone at the macOS window server level. This excludes the window from the frame buffer before any recording tool — Zoom, QuickTime, OBS, or any other — can access it. The result is that the overlay never appears in any recording or screen share, regardless of what software the observer uses.

## Key facts

- License: MIT (free and open source)
- Platform: macOS 14 Sonoma or later — universal binary (Apple Silicon + Intel)
- Install: brew tap Nainounen/blindspot && brew install --cask blindspot
- Source: https://github.com/Nainounen/blind-spot
- Releases: https://github.com/Nainounen/blind-spot/releases/latest

## Supported AI providers

- OpenAI (GPT models)
- Anthropic (Claude models)
- Google Gemini
- Ollama (local models, zero API cost)
